title: Behavioral drift
summary: The same contract is stretched to satisfy different consumers, and its meaning begins to drift even though structure still looks compatible.

services.summary: Now adapts behavior per consumer instead of preserving one consistent profile-normalization meaning.
Still expects the older profile semantics for downstream batch imports.

runtime_decisions: Detected multiple consumer-specific interpretations behind one contract boundary.
Allowed execution because no runtime rule rejects it, but flagged drift for operators.
Observed that compatibility now depends on consumer context instead of one shared meaning.
expectations: A contract can stay structurally stable while its behavior becomes less precise.
Behavioral drift is an architectural problem even before obvious breakage appears.
Manual coordination can notice drift, but it cannot reliably prevent it over time.
